message:
refactor(error): continue retiring anyhow::anyhow! wrappers in favor of typed BeadsError variants

Follow-up to the Upgrade variant refactor: three more call sites were
wrapping errors with `anyhow::anyhow!(...).into()`, which prevented
the structured error layer from attaching a meaningful error code or
context block.

- `cli::commands::create::generate_new_id`:
  The inner `id_exists` callback could not return `Result` through
  `id_gen.generate`'s existence predicate, so the previous code
  captured only the error's stringified message in a `Cell<String>`
  and re-wrapped it with `anyhow`. This flattened every storage error
  (database lock, schema mismatch, etc.) into an opaque "Other".

  Switch the capture to a `RefCell<Option<BeadsError>>` so the real
  typed error is preserved and returned verbatim if an existence
  check fails — CLI consumers now see the correct error code and
  retry semantics instead of a generic message.

- `cli::commands::version::fetch_latest_version`:
  Spawning curl, awaiting its exit status, and parsing its JSON
  response were each wrapped in anyhow. Replace with:
    * `BeadsError::external_command("curl", ...)` for spawn/wait
      failures, matching how other subprocess integrations report
      failures and enabling structured matching on command name.
    * `?` on `serde_json::from_str`, relying on the existing
      `From<serde_json::Error>` impl instead of re-wrapping.
    * `BeadsError::validation("GitHub response", "missing tag_name")`
      when the upstream payload is malformed, so this failure rides
      the validation code path (with the field name recorded) rather
      than being indistinguishable from a network error.

- `logging::init_logging` / `resolve_env_filter[_with_override]`:
  Switch the module's internal `Result` alias from `anyhow::Result`
  to `crate::error::Result`, and explicitly map the two trouble
  spots:
    * `tracing::subscriber::set_global_default` failure becomes
      `BeadsError::internal(...)` — this only happens if a global
      subscriber was already installed (an invariant bug, not a
      user-recoverable error).
    * `EnvFilter::try_new` failure on the fallback filter string
      becomes `BeadsError::Config(...)` because the filter strings
      come from configuration / env / defaults.

All three are ergonomic improvements to error reporting; no behavioral
changes on the happy path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
